justification: The plan correctly addresses all 7 ACs: collect-all error pattern (not fail-fast), AC5 conditional required (express + no saturday_delivery), AC6 conditional forbidden (standard + saturday_delivery present) as distinct checks, error shape exactly { field, code }, and tests organized per AC including AC7 multi-failure. However, this is a plan, not executed code — the agent asked 'Approve to implement?' rather than delivering working code and passing tests, which introduces risk that the implementation may diverge from the plan.

observations.text: Collect-all pattern explicitly called out and mapped to AC7
AC5 and AC6 are distinct checks with separate error codes (REQUIRED_FOR_EXPRESS vs NOT_APPLICABLE_FOR_STANDARD)
Error shape matches exact { field, code } spec
All 7 ACs enumerated in the plan with corresponding logic steps
Output is a plan awaiting approval, not a finished implementation with passing tests — no code was actually written or executed
AC7 test description mentions 'one describe block per AC' but doesn't explicitly confirm a multi-failure request test combining missing recipient_name AND negative weight
